channel.title: EVM on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in EVM on RaihnForge

item.title: Earned Value Management Applied
item.description:
TO: ADMG 477, Lesson 2 FROM: Joshua Keyes DATE: April 23, 2023 SUBJECT: Earned Value Management Applied

Information Gathering

To begin the project status evaluation, we must determine Planned Value (PV). This is done by taking the Budget At Completion (BAC), which is $4,000, and multiplying it by the completed (planned) percentile. The completed (planned) percentile is 17 months divided by 4 years (48 months). We can see that the project is close to being on track as it sits at 35.42% planned completion. This means that the Planned Value (PV) currently is $1,416.67.

item.title: Project Management Plan: Nurse Workstation Technology Refresh — Phase 1
item.description:
This is the cumulative ADMG 474 project management plan, written in the voice of the Project Manager for Central Medical Center (Ellensburg, WA). It is presented here lightly edited for the portfolio. Tables, budget figures, and matrix attachments are referenced in the original but not reproduced in this web view.

Introduction

The Nurse Workstation Refresh Phase 1 is a technology refresh that is necessary to maintain high quality standards, reduce future maintenance costs, increase security, and increase efficiency for the stakeholders of Central Medical Center. Conducting Phase 1 of the greater initiative will begin the facility-wide refresh, prioritizing increased quality to areas that represent our first-line interaction with clients. 101 nurse workstations on floors 3, 4, and 5 will be updated to the more current operating system, Windows 10. Included in this document are details in support of a clear understanding of constraints for Nurse Workstation Refresh Phase 1. Costs, timelines, and scope are crucial elements of achieving project success. The clarity in their concept, execution, and control are a priority and this document communicates the understanding of each within the organization.
